title: rootless: Rearrange setup of rootless containers ***CIRRUS: TEST IMAGES***
body:
In order to run Podman with VM-based runtimes unprivileged, the
network must be set up prior to the container creation. Therefore
this commit modifies Podman to:
  1. create a network namespace
  2. pass the netns persistent mount path to the slirp4netns
     to create the tap inferface
  3. pass the netns path to the OCI spec, so the runtime can
     enter the netns

Closes #2897

Signed-off-by: Gabi Beyer <gabrielle.n.beyer@intel.com>

comments.body:
Hi @gabibeyer. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

With this PR change, when running start/stop/restart for a container, would it make more sense to:
1. On stop, remove the persistent NetNS store, and then on start recreate the netns with slirp OR
2. Keep the existing netns, and have a new option in slirp4netns that is some sort of `--enter-ns` that skips the cloning of newnet, and enters and resets up the stored bind mount path.
Note: The stop container kills both the conmon and slirp4netns processes.
@mheon @AkihiroSuda @giuseppe  @amshinde
I think removing entirely on stop is safest - it's best to clean up all resources on container stop, so we don't risk leaking network namespace mounts, for example.
@mheon What could I do to get the latest version of slirp4netns to run with the tests in the CI? Also, thank you for all your help with everything!
@cevich We'd need to build new images for that, right? I believe there's a how-to somewhere in the repo

> Keep the existing netns, and have a new option in slirp4netns that is some sort of --enter-ns that skips the cloning of newnet,

Does `slirp4netns --netns-type=path /proc/self/ns/net` work?

> @cevich We'd need to build new images for that, right? I believe there's a how-to somewhere in the repo

Yes, edit `contrib/cirrus/packer/{fedora,ubuntu}_setup.sh`, [this is relatively easy to test](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/cirrus/README.md#test_build_cache_images_task-task).

Unfortunately I also find it referenced in `Dockerfile` (curiously though, not Dockerfile.fedora).  Those are re-built some time after a PR merges into master, so there's no (currently) automated way to build + verify them in a PR.  It can be accomplished by a bit of manual hoop-jumping and CI tic-tac-tow, but it's not pretty.  Let me know if you think this is needed, and I'll help out.

> > Keep the existing netns, and have a new option in slirp4netns that is some sort of --enter-ns that skips the cloning of newnet,
> 
> Does `slirp4netns --netns-type=path /proc/self/ns/net` work?

I may be doing it wrong, but not that I could get to work; it is failing at the clone newnet stage. Also, at this point we aren't inside the container so I think `self` would be referring to the host if I'm not mistaken.

Woah woah woah. I am pretty convinced that the deferred removal of network namespaces in stop is not a good thing. This is potentially breaking cleanup under the covers.

I don't feel comfortable with this in 1.5.0 as such. We need to either make a release branch and revert in there, or figure out if there are blockers to reusing network namespaces for rootless containers during restart.

@giuseppe @gabibeyer Will slirp4netns handle this properly, assuming we don't configure the network namespace twice - we just keep using it.
@mheon from what I hacked around with, it seems possible. I can submit a PR to slirp4netns, and have @AkihiroSuda review to see if it is the appropriate way of handling.

Ack, works for me.

For reference, we're going to make a new branch on this repo for 1.5.0, and revert these patches in the branch (not from master). Once this is fixed we can cut a 1.5.1 release from master, including these patches, for testing.
